solutions.solution:
(D) The figure in question may be labeled as shown in the following figure

Smallest triangles = ΔAEF, ΔEPR, ΔFSQ, ΔPRD, ΔRSD, ΔSQD = 6
Single triangles = ΔPRD, ΔQDC, ΔEFD = 3
Triangles formed with two triangles = ΔPSD, ΔQDC, ΔFDQ, ΔFDP = 4
Triangles formed with three or four triangles = ΔABC, ΔPDC, ΔBDE, ΔPQD = 4
Largest triangle = ΔABC = 1
∴Total triangles = 6 + 3 + 4 + 4 + 1 = 18

(D) The figure in question may be marked at different points as shown in the following figure

Smallest triangles = ΔNGQ, ΔQHR, ΔPDQ, ΔNDP, Δ JIC, ΔICK = 6
Single triangles = ΔEMJ, ΔKLF, ΔRIF, ΔEQI, ΔPHI, ΔPGI = 6
Triangle formed with two triangles = ΔAEI, ΔBHJ, ΔDQI, ΔDRJ, ΔCJK, ΔGHI, ΔDEI, ΔDIF = 9
Triangles formed with three triangles = ΔADC, ΔDBC = 2
Largest triangles = ΔABC, ΔEDF = 2
∴. Total triangles = 6 + 6 + 9 + 2 + 2 = 25

(D) There are total 27 triangles in the given figure.

From this figure it is clear that,
Triangles formed by single units = ΔABC, ΔBDE, ΔBCE, ΔCEF, ΔDGH, ΔDHE, ΔHEI, ΔEIF, ΔFIJ, ΔGKL, ΔGIH, ΔLHM, ΔHMI, ΔMIN, ΔINJ and ΔNJO i.e., 16 triangles.
Triangles formed by combining four triangles are ΔADF, ΔBGI, ΔCHJ, ΔDKM, ΔLEN, ΔMFO and ΔDFM i.e., 7 triangles.
Triangle formed by combining 9 triangles are ΔAGJ, ΔBKN and ΔCLO i.e., 3 triangles. One triangle ΔAKO formed by complete figure.
So, total number of triangles = 16 + 7 + 3 + 1 = 27

(D) The figure may be marked as shown in the following diagram

The triangles in the figures are as follows
Smallest triangles = ΔAFE, ΔEFC, ΔALN, ΔCOM, ΔNHG, ΔGHO, ΔLBP, ΔMQD, ΔIPJ, ΔJIQ = 10
Single triangles = ΔAFH, ΔCFH, ΔBJK, ΔDJK = 4
Triangles formed with two small triangles = ΔAEC, ΔNOH, ΔGJM, ΔPQJ, ΔECH, ΔAEH, ΔLJG = 7
Triangles formed with three or four small triangles = ΔAHC, ΔLMJ, ΔBDK = 3
∴. Total triangles = 10 + 4 + 7 + 3 = 24
